$schema_note: AEDA-AI expert system rules catalog. Declarative documentation of the rules implemented in aeda/engine/auto_selector.py. The execution engine remains Python; this file serves audit and documentation purposes. Conditions are expressed over DataProfile fields.

validated_by_legend.scientific_tutor_2026: Rule reviewed and confirmed by M.Sc. Yoelvis Bolanos-Alvarez (LEA-CEAC), 2026
validated_by_legend.design: Technical design decision grounded in the cited literature

rules.rationale: Variables in % and mg/kg cannot be mixed without scaling; magnitude differences distort distance-based methods (Hastie et al., 2009).
Closure constraint produces spurious correlations (Aitchison, 1982). CLR recommended but applied only on explicit user activation (apply_clr=True).
Succop et al. (2004): for non-Normal variables, censored values are imputed as LOD divided by the square root of two.
Succop et al. (2004): for Normal variables, censored values are imputed as LOD divided by two.
Median imputation is robust to skewed distributions typical of trace-element geochemistry (Reimann & de Caritat, 2017).
Structural missingness (variables measured only in subsets by design) must not be imputed (Helsel, 2005); subset analysis is advised instead.
Median/IQR-based scaling is less sensitive to extreme values than z-score standardization (Pedregosa et al., 2011).
PCA is the reference linear technique for multicollinear geochemical matrices; 85% retained variance balances parsimony and information (Hastie et al., 2009).
UMAP preserves global structure better than t-SNE at lower computational cost (McInnes et al., 2018).
t-SNE has quadratic complexity in sample count; offered as an alternative only within its practical range (van der Maaten & Hinton, 2008).
With a high outlier proportion, forced-partition methods displace centroids; DBSCAN labels extremes as noise and preserves dense-region structure (Ester et al., 1996).
Clustering with K equal to the number of sampling sites tests the correspondence between chemistry and geography of the sampling design.
Ward dendrograms add exploratory value and do not require fixing K in advance (Hastie et al., 2009).
Automatic K via silhouette maximization (Rousseeuw, 1987) as a general-purpose alternative.
Cross-site comparison must be restricted to the surface fraction (0-10 cm; some authors use 0-5 or 0-20 cm) to avoid mixing historical periods. Per scientific tutor guidance.
Isolation Forest scales linearly and is robust to the skewed, multimodal distributions of trace-element data (Liu et al., 2008). Contamination estimated from univariate z-score outliers.
LOF detects samples anomalous relative to their local neighborhood, complementary to Isolation Forest's global view (Breunig et al., 2000).

Comparing Pearson vs Spearman flags monotonic non-linear associations. Threshold 0.6 consistent with compositional environmental data practice (Filzmoser et al., 2009) and confirmed by the scientific tutor as reasonable strength subject to significance testing.
Metal-grain size correlation assesses whether metal transport is controlled by fine fractions (clay adsorption), informing source identification.
Random Forest importance identifies which variables discriminate the detected groups, guiding geochemical interpretation.

rules.condition: has_heavy_metals == true AND reference_element_present == true AND has_depth == true
rules.rationale: Al as conservative lithogenic reference (Buat-Menard & Chesselet, 1979); deepest section per site approximates pre-anthropogenic baseline when radiometric dating is unavailable (Bolanos-Alvarez et al., 2024). Reference element user-configurable (Fe, Ti, Sc, Li).
NOAA SQuiRTs (Buchman, 2008) is a Quality Guideline (not Cuban regulation; none exists for sediment metals). Classification limited to metals present in both the dataset and the table.
Birch (2003) seven-band severity classification for EF communication and cross-study comparison.
Rudnick & Gao (2013) upper continental crust values as auxiliary natural-background reference. Added on scientific tutor recommendation.
